title: Pancreatic Islet RNA Extraction
author: Islet and Pancreas Analysis Core
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.b2r9qd96
materials: Discard LCM Additive from DNase Kit.

steps.title: RNAqueous Kit – Can be stored...
steps.instruction: RNAqueous Kit – Can be stored at 4 °C up to 6 months.

steps.title: DNase Kit – Can be stored...
steps.instruction: DNase Kit – Can be stored at -20 °C up to 6 months.

steps.title: Pick at least 75 islets into an RNase-free microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Pick at least 75 islets into an RNase-free microcentrifuge tube. Islets should be free of acinar tissue and other debris. For more information on picking islets with regard to size, refer to step 10 of the Static Incubation protocol: Static Incubation of Pancreatic Islets by Islet and Pancreas Analysis Core

steps.title: Centrifuge islets for 00:03:00 at 200 rcf, 4 °C,
steps.instruction: Centrifuge islets for 00:03:00 at 200 rcf, 4 °C, .

steps.title: Remove supernatant and add 1 mL cold RNase-free PBS
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and add 1 mL cold RNase-free PBS.

steps.title: Repeat steps 4-5 twice, for a...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 4-5 twice, for a total of 3 PBS washes.

steps.title: Remove supernatant, add 200 mL Lysis...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant, add 200 mL Lysis Solution , and vortex to mix. At this point, lysates can be stored at -80 °C for future RNA extraction. Islets can also be stored dry, but it is recommended by the kit protocol to freeze in Lysis Solution.

steps.title: Turn heat block to 75 °C
steps.instruction: Turn heat block to 75 °C . Pipet total required volume of Elution Solution for all samples into a microcentrifuge tube, cap tightly, and place in heat block. Use the table below to calculate volume needed per sample (each elution will be performed in two steps). A B C D E F 1 Sample IEQ 75 islets 100 islets 150 islets 200 islets 250+ islets 2 Elution steps 10 μL + 10 μL 15 μL + 10 μL 15 μL + 15μL 20 μL + 15 μL 20 μL + 20 μL 3 Total Elution Solution (μL) 20 25 30 35 40 4 Total samples 5 6 Total Elution Solution for all samples (μL) � Table 1: Recommended elution volumes. Copy and paste all cells above into an Excel sheet, then enter values into cells B4–F4. The total volume of Elution Solution required will be automatically returned in cell B6.

steps.title: Remove DNase kit from -20°C to thaw at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Remove DNase kit from -20°C to thaw at Room temperature .

steps.title: Add 100 µL 100% ETOH to...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL 100% ETOH to each lysate and vortex to mix. If lysates were previously frozen, continue vortexing until thawed.

steps.title: Prepare a Micro Filter Cartridge Assembly for each sample
steps.instruction: Prepare a Micro Filter Cartridge Assembly for each sample: insert a cartridge into a round-bottom microcentrifuge tube, and label both pieces.

steps.title: Pipet 150 µL of lysate/ETOH mixture into the cartridge
steps.instruction: Pipet 150 µL of lysate/ETOH mixture into the cartridge. Centrifuge for 00:00:10 at 18400 rcf, °C, to pass the lysate through the cartridge and bind the RNA to the filter.

steps.title: Repeat step 12 for the final 150 µL of lysate
steps.instruction: Repeat step 12 for the final 150 µL of lysate.

steps.title: Add 180 µL Wash Solution 1 to the cartridge
steps.instruction: Add 180 µL Wash Solution 1 to the cartridge. Centrifuge for 00:00:10 at 18400 rcf, °C, .

steps.title: Remove cartridge from tube and discard contents
steps.instruction: Remove cartridge from tube and discard contents. Replace cartridge back into tube.

steps.title: Add 180 µL Wash Solution 2/3 to the cartridge
steps.instruction: Add 180 µL Wash Solution 2/3 to the cartridge. Centrifuge for 00:00:10 at 18400 rcf, °C, .

steps.title: Repeat step 16 for a total...
steps.instruction: Repeat step 16 for a total of two washes with Wash Solution 2/3 .

steps.title: Centrifuge for 00:01:00 at 21300 rcf,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 00:01:00 at 21300 rcf, °C, to remove any residual liquid from the filter.

steps.title: Transfer cartridge to a new, prelabeled round-bottom microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Transfer cartridge to a new, prelabeled round-bottom microcentrifuge tube. The schematic below illustrates steps 20-22. Figure 1: RNA elution is performed in two steps, then RNA is transferred to a fresh microcentrifuge tube for DNase treatment.

steps.title: Add the first volume of pre-heated...
steps.instruction: Add the first volume of pre-heated Elution Solution to the cartridge filter. Centrifuge for 00:00:30 at 18400 rcf, °C, .

steps.title: Repeat step 20 with the second aliquot of Elution Solution
steps.instruction: Repeat step 20 with the second aliquot of Elution Solution. Remove and discard filter cartridge.

steps.title: Label an RNase-free microcentrifuge tube and...
steps.instruction: Label an RNase-free microcentrifuge tube and transfer the Elution Solution (now containing RNA) from the round-bottom microcentrifuge tube to the RNase-free tube.

steps.title: Add 5 µL DNase Buffer and...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL DNase Buffer and 1 µL DNase to each RNA sample. Vortex gently to mix.

steps.title: Incubate samples in 37 °C water bath for 00:20:00
steps.instruction: Incubate samples in 37 °C water bath for 00:20:00 .

steps.title: Add 5 µL Inactivation Reagent to...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL Inactivation Reagent to each sample, and vortex gently to mix.

steps.title: Incubate samples at 25 °C for...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples at 25 °C for 00:02:00 , vortexing gently after 1 minute of incubation, and then again after the incubation is complete.

steps.title: Centrifuge samples for 00:01:30 at 21300 rcf, °C,
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples for 00:01:30 at 21300 rcf, °C, .

steps.title: Being careful not to disturb the...
steps.instruction: Being careful not to disturb the pellet, pipet the supernatant from each sample into a new, prelabeled RNase-free tube.

steps.title: Assess the quality and concentration of...
steps.instruction: Assess the quality and concentration of RNA using the method of your choice. The IPA Core uses the services of the Vanderbilt University Medical Center VANTAGE Core .

steps.title: Add 10
steps.instruction: Add 10.5 mL 100% ETOH to Wash Solution 1 bottle. Mark and date the bottle.

steps.title: Add 22
steps.instruction: Add 22.4 mL 100% ETOH to Wash Solution 2/3 bottle. Mark and date the bottle.
